genre_refs: If You See Something, Say Something (DHS campaign slogan)
description: A designed DHS 'If You See Something, Say Something' public-awareness banner in wide landscape format. The right two-thirds is a photograph of a young bearded man in a purple/mauve hoodie over a blue shirt, riding public transit (a bus or train car with metal grab rails and blue seats), holding a smartphone to his ear and glancing warily to the side; other blurred passengers are visible behind him. The upper-left is a solid light-blue panel with white text: a small line 'Protect your every day.' above the campaign's signature mixed-weight wordmark 'If you see something, say something' (with 'see' and 'say' bolded). Overlaid across the lower-left photo area in bold white all-caps is 'REPORT SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY TO LOCAL AUTHORITIES.' Bottom-left shows the small 'SEE SAY' campaign logo and the DHS seal, with fine print: '"If You See Something, Say Something" used with permission of the NY Metropolitan Transportation Authority.' The messaging technique is civic-vigilance encouragement, presenting an ordinary commuter as a watchful citizen-reporter. Target audience: the general public/transit riders. Not immigration-enforcement themed.
notable_text: Protect your every day.; If you see something, say something; REPORT SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY TO LOCAL AUTHORITIES.
